Exploration Learning Books Misc

Books

Reviews, reading notes, and recommendations for mathematics books — from undergraduate primers to research-level texts.

Books

Review: Gödel, Escher, Bach — An Eternal Golden Braid

Douglas Hofstadter's labyrinthine meditation on self-reference, consciousness, and formal systems

Author Douglas R. Hofstadter Published 1979, Basic Books Pages 777 Rating ★★★★★ Best for Anyone with mathematical curiosity and patience

Mar 20, 2024 · 15 min read
Books

Review: Principles of Mathematical Analysis

Baby Rudin — the most influential, most hated, most loved analysis textbook ever written

Author Walter Rudin Published 1953 (3rd ed. 1976), McGraw-Hill Pages 342 Rating ★★★★☆ Best for Undergraduate analysts, graduate review

Feb 10, 2024 · 10 min read
Books

Review: The Man Who Knew Infinity

Robert Kanigel's biography of Srinivasa Ramanujan — self-taught genius, mystic, and mathematical phenomenon

Author Robert Kanigel Published 1991, Charles Scribner's Sons Pages 438 Rating ★★★★★ Best for Everyone — no technical background needed

Jan 30, 2024 · 8 min read
Books

Review: How to Solve It

Pólya's timeless guide to the art of mathematical problem solving

Author George Pólya Published 1945, Princeton University Press Pages 288 Rating ★★★★★ Best for Students, teachers, anyone who solves problems

Nov 5, 2023 · 7 min read
